title: Rhino Build-Plan IR
description: Validated intermediate representation (IR) shared by the text-to-model, image-to-model and rhino-scene-state skills. It externalizes the full spatial intent of a model so the LLM can re-read world state each step instead of holding it in context. Every numeric value is expressed in the document 'units' against the named 'world_frame'. Producers (text-to-model, image-to-model) write 'object'..'verify'; rhino-scene-state appends realized GUIDs out-of-band in the scene-graph artifact, not here. The build pipeline reads this IR to emit guarded geometry codegen and to drive the render+measure+repair loop.

object.description: Plain-language name of the thing being modeled, e.g. 'four-legged dining chair'. Used in verify binary_questions and in part provenance.

units.description: Document unit system. Must be set on sc.doc BEFORE any geometry is created. Every dims/origin/penetration/height number in this IR is in these units.

tolerance.description: Absolute model tolerance in 'units'. At runtime always prefer the live sc.doc.ModelAbsoluteTolerance; this value is the IR's intended tolerance for join/offset/boolean operations and for naked-edge checks.

world_frame.description: Named base construction plane that all part frames are relative to. Default WorldXY. Parts relocate from Plane.WorldXY to their target plane via Transform.PlaneToPlane.

scale.description: How absolute size was determined. Text pipeline typically value_source='stated' with high confidence; image pipeline typically 'reference_object' or 'metrology_assumption' with a range + medium/low confidence, which forces the verify loop onto scale-invariant ratio_checks only (correction C5).

value_source.description: stated = a real dimension was given in the prompt (verify absolutes). reference_object = scaled off a known object in an image (carry confidence). metrology_assumption = a category default was assumed (lowest trust).

overall_height_mm.description: Overall bounding height in millimetres. A single number when known, or a [min,max] inclusive range when scale is uncertain (image pipeline). Always mm regardless of document 'units'.

oneOf.description: [min, max] inclusive range; max must be >= min (enforced in producer).

confidence.description: Confidence in the absolute scale. low/medium => verify loop fires repairs on ratio_checks only, never on absolute numeric_checks.

provenance.description: Where the scale came from, e.g. 'prompt: 450mm seat height' or 'reference: A4 sheet 297mm in image'.

assumption.description: The explicit assumption made when value_source='metrology_assumption', e.g. 'standard dining chair seat height ~450mm'.

symmetry.description: Declared symmetries of the object. Used both to reduce part authoring (mirror/array) and to generate symmetry verify questions.

plane.description: Mirror plane, e.g. 'WorldYZ' or a named frame. Required when type='mirror'.

axis.description: Rotational axis, e.g. 'WorldZ'. Required when type='rotational'.

origin.description: Origin point of the symmetry plane/axis in 'units' against world_frame.

count.description: Number of instances around a rotational axis (optional, rotational only).

params.description: Named scalar parameters that parts/relations reference symbolically (e.g. 'seat_height', 'leg_radius'). Keeps proportions consistent and makes ratio_checks computable. All values are plain numbers in 'units'.

stages.description: Ordered build STAGES for scoped, idempotent, checkpointed emit. Each stage names a slice of 'parts' (by part id) that is baked + reconciled as one unit, then checkpointed, before the next stage runs. A part with no stage belongs to an implicit 'default' stage. Stages exist so the double-execution wrapper self-corrects WITHIN a stage (the stage deletes its own objects by tag, then re-creates them once) and so a fragile-op failure or a later edit rebuilds only its stage, never the whole model (see conventions §12).

id.description: Stable stage id. Stamped as UserString 'stage' on every object baked in this stage and used as the scoped delete key for idempotent re-emit (conventions §12).

parts.description: Ordered part ids built in this stage (a subset of the top-level 'parts'). The same id must not appear in two stages.

depends_on.description: Stage ids that must be baked + checkpointed before this stage (e.g. a 'bell_chamber' stage depends_on 'shaft'). Used to order emit and to know which downstream stages to re-verify after a re-emit.

parts.description: Ordered list of parts. Each part is EITHER a primitive (has 'primitive') OR an operation (has 'operation'), never both. Operations may consume earlier parts by id via their input fields.

boolean_plan.description: Explicit, ordered boolean execution plan. Each step names an op, the input part ids, and the id of the resulting part. Mirrors any boolean operation parts but gives the executor a single linear sequence to run and to volume-check (correction C2).

inputs.description: Part ids consumed by this boolean. For difference, inputs[0] is the base; the rest are subtracted.

result.description: Part id of the boolean result.

verify.description: Acceptance criteria evaluated by render-and-look + analyze_objects. binary_questions and compare_to_reference go to vision (profile fidelity / 'looks like X'); numeric_checks and ratio_checks go to bbox/analyze math (vision-demotion, correction C4).

binary_questions.description: Yes/no questions answered by colored-part vision, e.g. 'is the red seat above the blue legs?'. Phrase so the answer depends on relative position/color, not measured size.

numeric_checks.description: Absolute measurements (only trustworthy when scale.confidence=high, i.e. text pipeline).

ratio_checks.description: Scale-invariant ratios (e.g. seat_height/overall_height). The ONLY checks the image pipeline may fire repairs on (correction C5).

compare_to_reference.description: When true, render-and-look diffs the rendered model against the reference image/silhouette (image pipeline).

point3d.description: [x, y, z] in document 'units' against world_frame.

point3d_expr.description: [x, y, z] where each coordinate is a numeric (a literal number OR a value_ref). Plain literal triples remain valid (existing examples). Used for attach origins that must resolve a coordinate from another part's published 'support' (e.g. a baluster top whose z is rail.z_at_angle), the PREVENT leg of §13.

value_ref.description: A NON-LITERAL numeric value resolved at Phase-3 codegen against the live build, not a hardcoded coordinate. This is the PREVENT leg of the connectivity triad (conventions §13/C9): a baluster top references the rail's realized helix-Z at its angle, so attach geometry resolves to the correct literal instead of a guessed number. Exactly one of {param, op, part} must be present. Resolution: 'param' looks up params[name]; 'op' folds args with the arithmetic operator; 'part' reads a realized support coordinate PUBLISHED by another part's 'support' block (or its baked solid) — 'of' selects which coordinate, 'at' (for z_at_angle) supplies the angle in degrees. A producer may leave value_refs symbolic in the IR; the executor must resolve every value_ref to a finite number BEFORE baking, and the resolved literal is what the connectivity sweep (§13) later measures against.

param.description: Name of a top-level params[] entry to substitute (e.g. 'seat_height'). Mutually exclusive with 'op' and 'part'.

op.description: Arithmetic operator folded left-to-right over 'args' (each a number or nested value_ref). For '-' and '/', args[0] is the minuend/numerator. Mutually exclusive with 'param' and 'part'.

args.description: Operands for 'op'; each is itself a numeric (number OR value_ref), so expressions nest (e.g. rail_base_z + pitch*turns).

part.description: Part id whose realized support coordinate this value reads (the PREVENT resolution of an attach point). Pairs with 'of'. Mutually exclusive with 'param' and 'op'.

of.description: Which coordinate of the referenced part to read. 'top_z'/'base_z' are the published plane heights (part.support); 'z' is the frame-origin z; 'centroid_z' is the realized solid centroid z; 'z_at_angle' is the helix/arc Z at the angle given by 'at' (degrees) — for helical/curved supports this is the A4 realized-arc-length height, NOT a face label.

at.description: Angle in degrees (number or value_ref) for of='z_at_angle' on a helical/radial support; ignored otherwise.

numeric.description: Any IR numeric field: a plain literal number (keeps every existing example valid) OR a value_ref object resolved at codegen (conventions §13). Use this wherever dims/origins/heights/penetration/tol live so an attach coordinate can be published-by/derived-from another part instead of guessed.

frame.description: Explicit construction plane a part is built on. The part is authored on Plane.WorldXY then moved to this frame via Transform.PlaneToPlane.

origin.description: Frame origin [x,y,z]; each coordinate may be a literal or a value_ref so an attach origin can resolve from another part's published 'support' (§13 PREVENT).

plane.description: Named target plane, e.g. 'WorldXY', 'WorldYZ', 'WorldXZ', or a custom frame name resolved by the executor. Default WorldXY when omitted.

x_axis.description: Optional explicit plane X axis vector.

y_axis.description: Optional explicit plane Y axis vector.

expect.description: Expected value: a number for numeric_checks, or a number/ratio for ratio_checks.

tol.description: Acceptance tolerance for |measured - expect|, in 'units' for numeric_checks or absolute ratio delta for ratio_checks.

axis_line.description: An axis expressed as two points (revolve axis, etc). Must be coplanar with the revolve profile (correction C7).

line.description: [[x0,y0,z0],[x1,y1,z1]] start and end of the axis.

support.description: A support surface/level a part PUBLISHES so OTHER parts can attach to it (the PREVENT leg of §13/C9): a rail publishes its helix law, a floor publishes its top plane, and a baluster's frame origin references it via a value_ref {part:'<this id>', of:'z_at_angle', at:<angle>}. The published support is also what the connectivity sweep treats as the intended attach surface; the DETECT leg still measures the realized solid-to-solid gap (A1/A4), so a wrong support does not produce a false pass.

kind.description: plane_z/top_z/base_z publish a single horizontal level ('value'); helix_z/z_at_angle publish a Z-law evaluated at an angle (see 'helix'), the realized-arc-length height used for helical/curved attach (A4) — NOT a face label.

value.description: The published level in 'units' for kind in {plane_z, top_z, base_z}; a numeric (literal OR value_ref).

helix.description: Parameters of the published Z-law for kind in {helix_z, z_at_angle}. Z at angle theta(deg) = base_z + pitch*((theta - start_angle)/360). 'radius' locates the law in plan so a value_ref of='z_at_angle' resolves the attach height at a member's angle (A4).

pitch.description: Z rise per full 360-degree turn, in 'units' (signed: positive rises).

radius.description: Plan radius of the helix/arc, in 'units'.

start_angle.description: Angle (degrees) at which Z == base_z. Default 0.

array.description: Declares that ONE part stands for a FAMILY of 'count' instances generated by a rule (radial/helical/linear). The generating rule is authoritative: on initial bake the connectivity sweep measures all N members; on re-emit checkpoints it samples first/middle/last + any previously flagged member, and only re-measures the full N when this generating rule changed (conventions §13, B2). Each instance is baked with UserString part_id '<id>#<i>' (0-based) so the scene-graph and edges remain part_id-keyed (C1).

kind.description: radial: 'count' copies rotated by 'angle_step' about 'axis' at 'radius'. helical: as radial but each step also rises by 'z_step' (or 'pitch'/360 * angle_step). linear: 'count' copies translated by 'step' along 'axis'.

count.description: Number of instances in the family.

axis.description: Array axis for radial/helical (rotation axis, e.g. 'WorldZ') or linear (translation direction). Default WorldZ for radial/helical.

angle_step.description: Per-instance rotation in degrees for radial/helical.

z_step.description: Per-instance Z rise for helical, in 'units' (alternative to deriving from 'pitch').

pitch.description: Helix Z rise per full 360-degree turn, in 'units' (z_step may be derived as pitch*angle_step/360).

step.description: Per-instance translation vector for linear arrays, in 'units'.

start_angle.description: Angle (degrees) of instance 0 for radial/helical. Default 0.

relation.description: A spatial/logical relation between this part and another. This is a CONNECTIVITY OBLIGATION, not just intent: at Phase 5/6 the connectivity sweep (conventions §13/C9) measures the REALIZED gap between the two live solids (by GUID, A1) and enforces a per-relation-type tolerance band (A3). 'penetration' is REQUIRED when type='interpenetrate' (parts feeding a boolean union must overlap 0.5-2mm, correction C3). Contact relations (on_top_of, lands_on, meets, coincident, spans, spans_between) participate in the completeness clause: every NON-floating assembly part must own >=1 measured contact or it is flagged UNCOVERED=FAIL.

type.description: Relation kind. Contact/support kinds and their §13 (A3) measured-gap bands: on_top_of => realized gap in [0,+tol], any penetration is a FAIL (A rests on top of B's surface); lands_on => A's base reaches B's realized top, gap in [-penetration,+tol] (a column landing on a floor, may seat slightly in); meets => two ends abut, gap in [-penetration,+tol]; coincident => |gap| <= tol; interpenetrate => gap must be NEGATIVE 0.5-2mm (C3, union overlap); spans => A bridges to B at both ends, both endpoint gaps in band; spans_between => A is supported at BOTH endpoints by 'to' and 'to2', each endpoint gap in band. symmetric_about / child_of are logical, not measured. For curved/helical supports use at_surface='realized' so the gap is measured to the nearest point on B's realized solid (A4), never a scalar face height.

this.description: Part id this relation is anchored on (defaults to the owning part).

to.description: The other part id (or symmetry/frame name for symmetric_about). For spans_between this is the FIRST support; the second is 'to2'.

to2.description: Second support part id for type='spans_between' (A is supported at both ends; both endpoint gaps are measured and must fall in band).

tol.description: Optional per-relation acceptance band half-width in 'units' for the §13 measured-gap check, OVERRIDING the document tolerance for this edge only (A3). Interpreted per the relation type's band (see 'type'). When omitted the executor uses the document ModelAbsoluteTolerance. A numeric so it can itself be derived (e.g. a fraction of a member radius).

at_surface.description: WHERE on the 'to' part the gap is measured (A3/A4). 'top'/'bottom' = the part's published support plane (axis-aligned, fast); 'nearest' = nearest point on B's realized solid via Brep.ClosestPoint; 'centerline' = to the member axis; 'realized' = REQUIRED for curved/helical/rotated supports — the gap is the realized solid-to-solid MinDistanceBetween (A4), Z-at-arc-length, NOT a face label. Defaults to 'nearest' (realized-solid measurement) so a check is never silently reduced to AABB arithmetic on a non-axis-aligned relation (A2).

penetration.description: Overlap depth in 'units' (typically 0.5-2mm) for interpenetrate relations feeding a boolean union, and the allowed negative-gap floor for lands_on/meets seating. Coincident/coplanar contact is degenerate and forbidden for unions. A numeric (literal OR value_ref).

part.description: A primitive part (has 'primitive') or an operation part (has 'operation'). Exactly one of those two keys must be present.

id.description: Stable part identifier. Becomes the UserString 'part_id' on the baked object and the scene-graph key mapping to its GUID (correction C1).

stage.description: Build stage this part belongs to (conventions §12). Stamped as UserString 'stage' at bake; the scoped idempotent delete key. If the top-level 'stages' array is present it is authoritative; this inline field is a shorthand for plans that don't need explicit stage ordering. A part with neither belongs to the implicit 'default' stage.

provenance.description: Why this part exists / where its shape came from (prompt phrase, reference silhouette, archetype profile).

primitive.description: Primitive type. 'interpolated_curve' builds a Curve.CreateInterpolatedCurve through control_points (used as loft sections / sweep rails / revolve generatrices).

frame.description: Construction frame for a primitive part.

dims.description: Primitive dimensions in 'units', e.g. box {x,y,z}, cylinder {radius,height}, sphere {radius}, cone {radius,height}, plane {x,y}. Keys depend on 'primitive'. Each value is a numeric (literal number OR value_ref), so e.g. a baluster height can resolve as rail.z_at_angle - base_z instead of a guessed literal (§13 PREVENT).

control_points.description: Ordered control/interpolation points for 'interpolated_curve' (and reusable as section/rail/generatrix geometry).

count.description: Number of instances of this part (e.g. 4 legs).

instances.description: Explicit per-instance frames when 'count' instances are not a simple array.

instances_at.description: Explicit instance origin points (shorthand for instances with default plane).

operation.description: Operation type. Each requires its own fields below.

generatrix.description: Part id of the profile curve to revolve (revolve). Must touch the revolve axis at start AND end, or be closed (correction C6).

axis.description: Revolve axis as a two-point line, coplanar with the generatrix (revolve).

angle_deg.description: Revolve sweep angle in degrees (revolve; 360 for full).

sections.description: Ordered part ids of section curves to loft through (loft). All must run the same direction and be seam-aligned (correction C7).

rail.description: Part id of the rail curve (sweep1). Must be G1 continuous (correction C7).

height.description: Extrusion distance in 'units' (extrude); a numeric (literal OR value_ref so the distance can resolve to a published support height, §13).

thickness.description: Shell wall thickness in 'units' (shell); a numeric (literal OR value_ref). Must be < min feature size; brep must be closed/capped before shelling (correction C6).

open_face.description: Face index, or list of face indices, left open by the shell (shell).

cap.description: Whether to cap the result into a closed solid (revolve/extrude/sweep1). Required before shell.

inputs.description: Part ids consumed by this operation (boolean/loft/extrude inputs). For boolean difference, inputs[0] is the base.

result.description: Part id of the operation result (defaults to this part's own id).

relations.description: Relations this part has to others. Union inputs must declare an 'interpenetrate' relation with a penetration depth (correction C3). Contact relations (on_top_of/lands_on/meets/coincident/spans/spans_between) are the measured connectivity obligations enforced by §13/C9: a non-floating part with no measured contact is UNCOVERED=FAIL.

support.description: A support surface/level this part PUBLISHES for others to attach to (§13 PREVENT). E.g. a floor publishes top_z, a helical rail publishes helix_z; attachers reference it via value_ref {part, of, at}.

array.description: Declares this part as a FAMILY of 'count' instances generated by a radial/helical/linear rule (§13 B2). Each instance bakes as part_id '<id>#<i>'.

floating.description: When true this part is INTENDED to float (pendant tip, cantilever end, free finial) and is EXEMPT from the §13/C9 contact-completeness rule, so it never generates a false UNCOVERED. A floating part may still declare measured relations; it just is not REQUIRED to own one.
